title: [Bug]: Priority default metadata accepts control characters
summary: Priority defaults are normalized with whitespace trimming but are not rejected when they contain embedded control characters.
observed: message_metadata.priority.default can contain embedded control characters and still become part of the effective runtime metadata policy.
expected: Priority defaults should reject control characters so metadata remains safe for logs, metrics, routing, and destination storage.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataFieldConfig(header='Priority', default='routine\u0000x') and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects priority defaults containing ASCII control characters to fail validation.
impact: Unsafe default metadata can propagate into all messages on a subject family and make downstream records difficult to inspect safely.
delivery_semantics: No ACK or sink success ordering behavior changes.
security: The fix must fail closed during configuration validation and avoid logging the unsafe value.
acceptance: Priority defaults reject all ASCII control characters.
Blank defaults continue to normalize to null.
The regression test covers an embedded control character.
Release notes describe stricter metadata default validation.
tests: Run focused unit tests and the full local validation script.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized validation evidence.
